title: populus candicans var. ontariensis
intro: Populus candicans var. ontariensis, also known as the Bigtooth Aspen, is a deciduous tree native to North America. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, derived from its bark, leaves, and roots. The Bigtooth Aspen has been used to treat various health issues, including sore throats, wounds, and fever, due to its analgesic and expectorant properties. Traditionally, it has been prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve for its herbal remedies, and it can be found in some herbal preparations, such as cough syrups and wound care products.
